assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that surge upgrade upgrades nodes in-place while blue-green creates entirely new nodes

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Response recommends surge upgrade instead of blue-green, despite the fact that local SSD data cannot migrate between nodes

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states that surge upgrade preserves local data and works in-place, missing the critical point that local SSD data is lost during node draining

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: Response provides surge upgrade configuration with maxSurge/maxUnavailable parameters instead of blue-green configuration with autoscaling and rollout policies

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the need to decommission nodes from the Cassandra ring or rebuild data on new nodes

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Response provides commands for configuring surge upgrade on existing node pool, not for creating a new node pool for blue-green deployment

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl drain and cordon commands in the rollback section

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudget configuration with maxUnavailable: 1 to protect Cassandra availability
